segments.text: Build an altar of acacia wood, three cubits high; it is to be square, five cubits long and five cubits wide.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Make a horn at each of the four corners, so that the horns and the altar are of one piece, and overlay the altar with bronze.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Make all its utensils of bronze—its pots to remove the ashes, and its shovels, sprinkling bowls, meat forks and firepans.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Make a grating for it, a bronze network, and make a bronze ring at each of the four corners of the network.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Put it under the ledge of the altar so that it is halfway up the altar.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Make poles of acacia wood for the altar and overlay them with bronze.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The poles are to be inserted into the rings so they will be on two sides of the altar when it is carried.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Make the altar hollow, out of boards. It is to be made just as you were shown on the mountain.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Make a courtyard for the tabernacle. The south side shall be a hundred cubits long and is to have curtains of finely twisted linen,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: with twenty posts and twenty bronze bases and with silver hooks and bands on the posts.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The north side shall also be a hundred cubits long and is to have curtains, with twenty posts and twenty bronze bases and with silver hooks and bands on the posts.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The west end of the courtyard shall be fifty cubits wide and have curtains, with ten posts and ten bases.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: On the east end, toward the sunrise, the courtyard shall also be fifty cubits wide.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Curtains fifteen cubits long are to be on one side of the entrance, with three posts and three bases,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: and curtains fifteen cubits long are to be on the other side, with three posts and three bases.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For the entrance to the courtyard, provide a curtain twenty cubits long, of blue, purple and scarlet yarn and finely twisted linen—the work of an embroiderer—with four posts and four bases.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: All the posts around the courtyard are to have silver bands and hooks, and bronze bases.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The courtyard shall be a hundred cubits long and fifty cubits wide, with curtains of finely twisted linen five cubits high, and with bronze bases.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: All the other articles used in the service of the tabernacle, whatever their function, including all the tent pegs for it and those for the courtyard, are to be of bronze.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Command the Israelites to bring you clear oil of pressed olives for the light so that the lamps may be kept burning.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: In the tent of meeting, outside the curtain that shields the ark of the covenant law, Aaron and his sons are to keep the lamps burning before the Lord from evening till morning. This is to be a lasting ordinance among the Israelites for the generations to come.
segments.speaker: God
